name: New Haven Bank
description_short: New Haven Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank operating in Connecticut since 2010, offering checking, savings, CDs, and commercial lending with a commitment to underserved communities.
description_long:
New Haven Bank was established in 2010 and has positioned itself as a local banking alternative with national recognition. The bank is FDIC-insured and maintains a Bauer Financial 5-Star Rating for financial strength and stability, along with an A+ rating from DepositAccounts.com. As Connecticut's only CDFI (Community Development Financial Institution) bank, New Haven Bank focuses on serving traditionally underserved populations and communities, distinguishing itself from larger regional and national banks.

The bank offers comprehensive personal and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, online and mobile banking, telephone banking, personal and business credit cards, and commercial lending. For businesses, they provide SBA loans, cash management solutions, and a dedicated commercial lending team. Current deposit rates include a 2.70% APY Safe Haven Savings Account and CD rates ranging from 3.45% to 3.55% APY across different terms. They also partner with community organizations on BankOn initiatives to provide affordable, low-cost banking options.

What distinguishes New Haven Bank is its explicit community focus and CDFI designation. The bank emphasizes "local banking" with expertise rooted in the New Haven area, markets itself around 15 years of local experience, and has won "Best Bank/Credit Union" in the Greater New Haven Reader's Poll. They actively reinvest profits back into the community and position themselves as advocates for economic development in underserved areas. The routing number is 011110756.

As a smaller regional bank, New Haven Bank's primary limitation is geographic scope—they operate specifically in the New Haven/Connecticut area, making them inaccessible to customers outside this region. While they offer competitive deposit rates and strong community credentials, their service footprint and product breadth are narrower than national banks. Customers seeking nationwide branch access, extensive investment products, or premium wealth management may need to look elsewhere. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with various terms
Telephone banking for account access and customer service
Cash management services for business customers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government; Bauer Financial 5-Star rating for financial strength
Only CDFI bank in New Haven—explicitly serves underserved communities and promotes economic development
Competitive deposit rates: 2.70% APY on savings accounts and 3.45-3.55% APY on CDs
Specialized SBA loan program with local commercial lending team for small business support
BankOn partnership provides affordable, low-cost banking options for financially underserved customers
Online and mobile banking available with 24/7 account access; telephone banking option for personal service
Local decision-making and relationship-focused approach; won Best Bank/Credit Union in Greater New Haven Reader's Poll
cons: Geographic limitation: only operates in Connecticut/New Haven area; not available to out-of-state customers
Limited branch and ATM network compared to major regional and national banks
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or premium banking tiers that larger institutions offer
Smaller institution means fewer specialized services like private banking, trust services, or business advisory
Less brand recognition outside New Haven region despite national recognition for financial stability
best_for: Small business owners and startups seeking SBA loans with local relationship-based lending
New Haven-area residents wanting community-focused banking with CDFI mission alignment
Underserved populations seeking affordable, accessible banking through BankOn partnership
Local entrepreneurs and growing businesses needing cash management and commercial lending expertise
diagnosis: New Haven Bank is best for Connecticut-based small business owners, local entrepreneurs, and New Haven-area residents seeking community-focused banking with genuine relationship-building and a demonstrated commitment to underserved communities. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank operates exclusively in the New Haven area and is not accessible to customers outside Connecticut.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: New Haven Bank is an FDIC-insured bank in Connecticut offering checking, savings, CDs, and commercial lending to underserved communities.
